title: fix: jsxDEV is not a function in production (gate dev mode on NODE_ENV)
description: Deployed app throws TypeError: jsx_dev_runtime.jsxDEV is not a function. Cause: Bun.serve had development:{hmr,console} hardcoded, forcing the dev JSX runtime even under NODE_ENV=production where React's jsx-dev-runtime isn't bundled. Fix: enable development only when NODE_ENV !== production.
notes: Fixed. index.ts: development gated on NODE_ENV (dev object only when NODE_ENV!=production, else false). Production now uses stable react/jsx-runtime; served bundle has 0 jsxDEV refs. Service restarted; local 200; chunk asset serves 200. Root cause was hardcoded development:{hmr,console}.

title: feat: Bridge page — type two chords, get ranked bridges, play sequence
description: New nav tab: from/to inputs -> bridge cards (name + sequence diagrams + play whole sequence). Sequence strum playback.
notes: Done. BridgePage: from/to inputs -> bridge cards (name + sequence of mini diagrams with arrows + play-whole-sequence via strumSequence). Bridge nav tab added. strumSequence helper in audio.ts.

title: feat: chord bridge generator (secondary dom, ii-V, dim, tritone...)
description: generateBridges(from,to) -> ranked short sequences [from..to] named by theory: secondary dominant, ii-V, leading-tone dim7, tritone sub, backdoor, walking bass, suspension. Deduped, ~12 cap, only playable chords. Pure/TDD.
notes: Done. bridge.ts generateBridges(from,to) -> ranked [from..to] sequences: secondary dominant, ii-V, dom9, leading-tone dim7, suspension, tritone sub, backdoor, walking bass. Deduped, cap 12, only voiceable chords. D->G yields D7 / Am7-D7 / F#dim7 / D/F# etc. TDD 8 tests.

title: fix: penalize muted inner strings (hard to mute while strumming)
description: Per Dimas: a muted inner string (hole) is hard to deaden cleanly while strumming; an open string that rings through is fine. Raise innerMutes weight 1->3 so x24432 (barre, no hole) ranks easier than x24x32 (muted G). Regression test added; SOT corrected.
notes: Fixed. innerMutes weight 1->3 (a muted inner string is hard to deaden while strumming; open 'hole' that rings is fine — feature only counts muted inner strings). Verified: x24432 (8.8) < x24x32 (9.8); x B D x B F# still easiest (5.4). Regression test + SOT corrected. 100 tests pass.

title: refactor: difficulty model — barre = 1 finger (easy-medium) + playability filter
description: Per Dimas: a compact barre (<=3 notes after, span<=2) is easy-medium, not hard. Rework scorer to count effective fingers (barre=1) with a modest barre surcharge; inner-mute penalty lowered (muting a middle string is easy). Add playability filter: open string can't ring with notes above fret 4. Result: Bm barre x24432 now ranks in top-20 shapes; junk open+highfret voicings removed.
notes: Done. VoicingFeatures adds 'fingers' (barre=1 finger + notes above it; else frettedCount). difficulty.ts: DEFAULT_WEIGHTS now {barre:2, fingers:1, fretSpan:1, innerMutes:1, position:0.2}; compact barre easy-medium. voicing.ts validity: open string can't coexist with fretted note > fret 4 (playability). Verified: Bm easiest = x B D x B F# (3.4), barre Bm x24432 = 8.8 rank 15/74 (was 85/142). SOT §7/§8 updated. 99 tests pass.

title: feat: Shapes page — all voicings of one chord, easiest first
description: A 🔄 button on Find + Library cards opens a Shapes page showing all voicings of that exact chord (topK 20, easiest first, incl barre shapes). Distinct from reharmonization 'variations'. Each shape: diagram + notes + play + save.
notes: Done. ShapesPage (topK 20 voicings of one chord, easiest first) reachable via 🔄 button on Find + Library cards; App routes shapesFor state, navbar clears it. Term chosen: 'Shapes' (vs voicings). Each shape: diagram+notes+play+save. With the difficulty rework, Bm shapes now lead with easy partials and include the barre.

title: fix: Stop should silence immediately mid-sequence
description: Pressing Stop only prevents future loop cycles; notes already scheduled in the current cycle keep playing. Track active oscillators and stop them (and cancel pending ones) immediately on stop.
notes: Fixed. audio.ts tracks active oscillators in a Set; stopAll() ducks master then osc.stop(now+0.04) on all (cuts ringing AND pending-this-cycle notes that haven't started), clears set. player.stop() calls stopAll(). Stop now silences immediately mid-sequence.

title: feat: wire play buttons through pattern player + Stop bar
description: SOT §11. All play buttons (Find/Library/Finder) play via the pattern player using global settings; floating Stop while looping.
notes: Done. All play buttons (Find/Library/Finder) call usePlayer().play -> pattern player; Settings nav tab added; floating Stop pill shown while looping; app wrapped in PlayerProvider.

title: feat: global Settings (pattern, tempo, mode) + Settings page
description: SOT §11. localStorage settings {patternName,bpm,loop}; Settings nav page: preset picker w/ grid preview, tempo slider, once/loop toggle.
notes: Done. PlayerContext (settings from localStorage + play/stop/isPlaying via player); SettingsPage: preset list with |cell| grid preview, tempo slider (40-200), once/loop toggle, Preview(D) + Stop. settings persistence in settings.ts (committed with 03h).

title: feat: Web Audio pattern sequencer (once/loop/stop)
description: SOT §11. Play a schedule once or looped; stop; X damps ringing. Refactor audio to pluck note subsets at scheduled times.
notes: Done. audio.ts refactored: master gain + pluckMidiAt(midi,when,strum) + muteAt (damps master) + playVoicing one-shot. player.ts PatternPlayer: play(frets,settings) builds schedule, plays once or loops (schedules cycle-by-cycle ahead, stop prevents future cycles), onState listeners for isPlaying. settings.ts included here (player imports the type). Verified bundles + 99 tests pass.

title: feat: pattern scheduler (pattern+voicing+bpm -> timed events)
description: SOT §11. buildSchedule pure (TDD): bass=lowest sounded string, treble=rest; T/F/A/X -> events {time,midi[],mute}; step=(60/bpm)/4s; 32 steps.
notes: Done. src/chord/scheduler.ts (TDD): buildSchedule(pattern,frets,bpm) -> {events:{time,midi[],mute},duration}. bass=lowest sounded (T), treble=rest (F), A=all, X=mute; step=(60/bpm)/4s; 32 steps. Verified timing + note split.

title: feat: pattern model + parser (strokes, presets)
description: SOT §11. Stroke type (. T F A X), Pattern{name,steps[32]}, parsePattern('|A...|...') (TDD), preset patterns p1/p2/p3.
notes: Done. src/chord/patterns.ts (TDD): Stroke/Pattern, parsePattern('|A...|') -> 32 strokes (pipes/ws ignored, validates chars+length), presets p1 Strum / p2 Thumb–Fingers / p3 Travis, presetByName.

title: feat: interactive chord finder (build a voicing, name the chord)
description: Interactive fretboard: tap strings/frets to build a voicing; app identifies/names the chord from the sounded notes (reverse of generation). Pure nameChord(pcs,bass) (TDD) + InteractiveFretboard component + optional save to library.
notes: Done. nameChord(pcs,bass) (TDD, 11 tests) identifies chords incl slash inversions and no-5th 7/9 voicings; InteractiveFretboard (tap to build voicing) + FinderPage (name it, play, save). Finder limited to first 5 frets v1. Browser-verify on device.

title: feat: Bootstrap navbar — separate Variations / Library / Finder pages
description: Split the single page into navbar-driven pages: Find (type chord -> variations), My Library, and Chord Finder. Use Bootstrap navbar; SPA tab state.
notes: Done. Bootstrap navbar with Find / My Library / Chord Finder tabs; App orchestrates shared library state; monolithic App split into FindPage/LibraryPage/FinderPage. Browser-verify on device.

title: fix: major-triad variations miss the dominant 9 (e.g. A9)
description: generateVariations('A') offers maj7/7/6/add9/maj9/sus2/sus4 but not A9 (dominant ninth). Since the dominant 7 is offered, the dominant 9 should be too — and 9 chords are a core use case. Add '9' to the major-triad default set (SOT §7) and update docs.
notes: Fixed. Added dominant 9 ('9') to major-triad variation set in variations.ts; SOT §7 updated. generateVariations('A') now includes A9; A9 resolves to a valid voicing. TDD red->green, 73 tests pass.

title: chore: deploy chords at chords.dimsky.xyz (systemd + Caddy)
description: Run the Bun app as a systemd service on 127.0.0.1:3300 (data at data/chords.db) and reverse-proxy chords.dimsky.xyz -> localhost:3300 via Caddy. DNS already resolves to this VPS.
notes: Done & verified live. systemd chords.service (User=dimas, PORT=3300, BIND_HOST=127.0.0.1, CHORDS_DB_PATH=.../data/chords.db, bun index.ts, Restart=always, enabled). Caddy block chords.dimsky.xyz -> localhost:3300 (Caddyfile backed up to Caddyfile.bak-pre-chords). index.ts now binds 127.0.0.1 via BIND_HOST. Verified: service active, listening 127.0.0.1:3300, local 200; LE cert obtained; https://chords.dimsky.xyz/ -> 200, /api/library -> [], HTTP->HTTPS 308. Deploy steps in docs/deploy.md.

title: test: on-device verification of Phase 1 UI
description: Browser-verify on phone (per project rule: components verified via browser automation/manual). Run: bun run dev (or deploy to chords.dimsky.xyz). Check: (1) typing a chord shows variation cards; (2) each card's chord diagram renders correctly (dots, O/X, finger numbers, up-neck position label); (3) play button strums audible chord; (4) save adds to My Library and persists across reload; (5) delete removes it; (6) layout is comfortable on a narrow phone screen. File bugs as new issues.

title: feat: note resolver
description: SOT 8. Voicing -> concrete pitches for audio. AC: open D voicing resolves to expected pitch set. TDD.
notes: Done. src/chord/noteResolver.ts TDD. resolveMidi(frets) -> sounded MIDI pitches low->high (open D xx0232 -> [50,57,62,66]); midiToFreq (A4=440); resolveFrequencies for the audio layer. Reuses OPEN_MIDI exported from voicing.ts. No drift.

title: feat: difficulty comparator + topK
description: SOT 7. score=weights.features (barre+5, fretted+1, span+1, inner-mute+2, position+0.2). Expose compare(a,b); topK(chord,10) returns easiest-first. AC: compare(open,barre) ranks open easier; open D < F barre. Built as weights.features so weights swappable in P1.5. TDD.
notes: Done. src/chord/difficulty.ts TDD. score=weights.features with SOT §7 DEFAULT_WEIGHTS (barre5/fretted1/span1/innerMute2/position0.2); verified open D=4.6, F barre=13.6. compare(a,b) comparator; topK(chord,k) returns k easiest valid voicings easiest-first; easiest(chord) returns the single easiest. Weights are a parameter (default), so P1.5 learned weights drop in with zero rework. No drift.

title: feat: from-scratch voicing generator + validity
description: SOT 7. Build guitar voicings by fretboard search (E A D G B E; mute/open/fret; fretted notes within 5-fret span; up to ~fret 12). Keep only valid voicings: essential tones (triad 1-3-5; 7th/9th may drop 5th), root/slash-bass lowest, >=4 sounding strings, doubling allowed. AC: open D/open C appear; missing-3rd or <4-string shapes rejected. TDD.
notes: Done. src/chord/voicing.ts (+ shared src/chord/chordTones.ts) TDD. chordTones maps quality+extensions -> pitch-class set + essential tones + root/bass pc. voicing.ts: fretboard search (standard tuning, frets 0-12), keeps only valid shapes per SOT §7 (essential tones present, bass=lowest string, >=4 sounding, fretted within 5-fret span). Each Voicing carries notes[] + features{hasBarre,frettedCount,fretSpan,innerMutes,position} for the comparator. Verified open D (xx0232) and open C (x32010) are generated; features match SOT sanity (open D fretted=3/span=1/no-barre). hasBarre heuristic = frettedCount>4 (reproduces SOT's open-D≈4.6 vs F-barre≈13.6). chordTones added here (shared, also used by note-resolver) rather than a separate issue. No drift.

title: feat: variation generator (reharmonization)
description: SOT 7 default variation set. Given parsed chord, auto-generate curated variations (maj7/7/6/add9/maj9/sus2/sus4 + /3,/5 for major; m7/m9/m6/madd9/sus2/sus4 + /b3,/5 for minor; 9/13/7sus4 for dom7), plus base chord. AC: variations('Bm') includes Bm7,Bm9,Bmadd9 and slash forms; list stays tight. TDD.
notes: Done. src/chord/variations.ts (+ shared src/chord/notes.ts helper) with TDD. Generates SOT §7 default set: major (maj7/7/6/add9/maj9/sus2/sus4 + /3,/5), minor (m7/m9/m6/madd9/sus2/sus4 + /b3,/5), dom7 (9/13/7sus4); base + typed chord always included, deduped, tight. Added notes.ts (note<->semitone, transposeNote) as a shared dependency also needed by voicing/note-resolver — documented here rather than a separate issue. No SOT drift.

title: feat: chord symbol parser
description: SOT 3 (Layer 1). Parse text chord symbols (Bm, C/E, Dmaj7, F#m7b5) into {root, quality, extensions[], bass?}. AC: parser tests for those symbols pass; invalid input rejected clearly. TDD.
notes: Done. Pure module src/chord/parser.ts + parser.test.ts (14 tests, TDD red->green). Parses root(+#/b), triad quality (maj/min/dim/aug/sus2/sus4), extensions longest-first (maj7/add9/7/b5/...), and /bass; rejects invalid input. Matches SOT §3 Layer 1 + data model. No drift.

title: feat: Phase 1 — chord engine + library
description: Umbrella for Phase 1 per docs/phases/phase-1.md: type a chord -> reharmonized variations -> easiest playable shape (diagram) -> hear it -> save to library. Pure-logic modules are TDD; UI is browser-verified.

title: feat: bridge connectors inherit extension level (jazzy in, jazzy out)
description: Connectors now take the richer of from/to color: triad/7 -> 7ths; 9 -> 9th dominants + m9 ii; 13 -> 13ths. D9->G9 yields Am9->D9, G#9, F9; plain D->G unchanged. TDD.

title: fix: drop no-op bridge connectors (e.g. D9 → D9 → G9)
description: When a bridge connector equals an endpoint (extended chords like D9→G9 where the dom9 connector == from-chord), the sequence was a no-op. Collapse consecutive duplicates and drop bridges with no real connector.

title: fix: dim7 should be fully-diminished (bb7), not m7b5
description: chordTones treats dim+7 as +10 (m7b5). A true dim7 (e.g. leading-tone F#dim7 for bridging) needs +9 (bb7). Fix so dim quality + 7 -> {0,3,6,9}.
notes: Fixed. chordTones: dim quality + '7' -> +9 (bb7, fully-diminished) not +10; dim b5 kept essential even with extensions (dim is a stack of minor 3rds). F#dim7 = F# A C Eb. TDD.

title: fix: parser rejects capital-case qualities (DMaj9, ASus4)
description: parseChord is case-sensitive on quality keywords; DMaj9 fails ('Maj9' unrecognised) though Dmaj9 works. Normalize maj/sus/dim/aug/add case-insensitively (roots stay uppercase, m=minor preserved).
notes: Fixed. parser.ts normalizes multi-letter quality keywords (maj/sus/dim/aug/add) to lowercase before parsing, so DMaj9/ASus4/CMAJ7 work; single 'm' (minor) + accidentals untouched, roots stay uppercase. TDD red->green.

title: fix: chord diagram overflows/drifts right in card (not centered)
description: ChordDiagram used a fixed px width larger than the 2-col card on phone, so it overflowed right. Make the SVG responsive: width=100%, maxWidth via style, block + margin auto to center.
notes: Fixed. ChordDiagram svg now width=100% with style maxWidth + display block + margin auto, so it scales to the card and centers (was fixed px wider than the 2-col card).

title: fix: note labels not aligned with string lines in diagram
description: The sounding notes were a joined text line under the SVG, not aligned to each string. Move them inside the SVG at the foot of each string column (aligned to stringX).
notes: Fixed. Note labels moved into ChordDiagram SVG, rendered at the foot of each string column aligned to stringX (blue, non-muted only); removed the joined-notes line from ChordCard. showNotes prop (default true).

title: fix: chord diagram crops the position label (e.g. 5fr)
description: The 'Nfr' position label on up-neck shapes is cut off at the SVG left edge. Increase left margin/viewBox and card padding so it isn't cropped.
notes: Fixed. ChordDiagram left margin 26->40 and position-label tuned so 'Nfr' no longer clips; ChordCard uses p-3. Browser-verify on device.

title: feat: show sounded note names under each chord diagram
description: Below each diagram, list the notes actually playing (the non-muted strings), e.g. 'D A D F#'. Pure helper resolveNoteNames(frets) (TDD) + render in card.
notes: Done. resolveNoteNames(frets) (TDD) returns sounded note names low->high; ChordCard renders them under the diagram (e.g. 'D A D F#').

title: feat: add-chord flow UI
description: SOT 3 (Layer 2). Type chord -> variation pick-list each with diagram of easiest shape + play button -> save chosen to library -> library list with delete. Browser-verified.
notes: Done. src/ui/App.tsx + api.ts: type chord -> generateVariations -> easiest() voicing per variation -> card grid with ChordDiagram + play + save; My Library section with diagram + play + delete (GET/POST/DELETE). Error handling for unparseable input. Bundles & serves clean; on-device interaction check pending.

title: feat: Bun.serve app shell + Bootstrap
description: SOT 7. Bun.serve HTML import + React + Bootstrap CSS, phone-first shell. Browser-verified.
notes: Done. index.ts Bun.serve: '/' serves src/ui/index.html (Bootstrap CDN, viewport meta, phone-first), /api/library GET/POST/DELETE backed by sqlite at data/chords.db (CHORDS_DB_PATH; mkdir data/). POST validates via parseChord. Verified by curl: GET/POST/invalid-400/DELETE-204 + frontend bundles & serves 200. dev/start/test scripts added.

title: feat: chord library persistence (sqlite)
description: SOT 8. bun:sqlite store for LibraryEntry {id,symbol,parsedChord,chosenVoicing,createdAt}. CRUD. TDD the data layer.
notes: Done. src/library/store.ts TDD (in-memory sqlite). initLibrary/addEntry/listEntries/deleteEntry; LibraryEntry={id,symbol,voicing[],createdAt}; voicing stored as JSON, list newest-first by id DESC. Stores symbol+chosen voicing (parsedChord derivable from symbol). No drift.

title: feat: chord diagram SVG component
description: SOT 7. SVG fretboard diagram from voicing frets[6]/fingers[6]: string/fret grid, finger dots+numbers, O/X open/muted, position label. Browser-verified.
notes: Done. src/ui/ChordDiagram.tsx (SVG: string/fret grid, finger dots+numbers, O/X markers, position label for up-neck shapes) + pure src/chord/fingering.ts helper (TDD). Renders from voicing frets. Bundles clean. On-device visual check pending (see verification issue).

title: feat: Web Audio chord playback
description: SOT 7. Synthesized strum of a chord's notes via Web Audio. Tap to hear. Browser-verified.
notes: Done. src/ui/audio.ts Web Audio synthesized strum (one decaying triangle osc + lowpass per string, staggered for strum) via resolveFrequencies. Bundles clean. On-device audio check pending.

title: chore: remove from/to placeholders on Bridge page
description: The 'D'/'G' placeholders read like pre-filled values; remove them.

title: chore: clean up production asset path (/../../chunk.js)
description: In production Bun emits the client script as src=/../../chunk-*.js because index.html is nested at src/ui/. Browsers normalize it to /chunk-*.js (serves 200, app works), but the path is ugly/fragile. Consider moving index.html to project root or configuring a public path so the emitted path is clean.
